title: E-Valued Causal Discovery: Constraint-Based Structure Learning with Anytime-Valid FDR Control
description: We propose E-PC, a constraint-based causal discovery algorithm that replaces p-value-based conditional independence tests with e-value-based tests and applies the e-BH procedure for graph-level false discovery rate control. Unlike existing approaches that require conservative corrections for test dependence, e-values provide FDR control under arbitrary dependence with no correction needed, yielding higher power and anytime validity.
motivation: Constraint-based causal discovery performs many dependent conditional independence tests but lacks principled graph-level FDR control. The existing solution (PC-p with Benjamini-Yekutieli correction) is overly conservative, losing substantial power, especially for large graphs. E-values, a recently formalized alternative to p-values, offer FDR control under arbitrary dependence without conservatism—a perfect match for the heavily dependent CI tests in causal discovery. Despite the recent explosion of e-value methodology, no work has applied e-values to causal graph structure learning.
proposed_approach: Three key components: (1) E-value CI tests using split-likelihood ratios (data is split into estimation and evaluation halves to construct valid e-values for conditional independence); (2) Sequential evidence accumulation across K data folds, where e-values are multiplied across folds for increasing power with anytime validity; (3) Graph-level FDR control via the e-BH procedure applied to edge-specific e-values after the PC skeleton phase. The resulting E-PC algorithm maintains the combinatorial constraint-based framework of PC while providing rigorous, non-conservative FDR guarantees.

related_work.title: Estimating and Controlling the False Discovery Rate of the PC Algorithm Using Edge-Specific P-Values
related_work.authors: Eric V. Strobl, Peter L. Spirtes, Shyam Visweswaran
related_work.venue: ACM Transactions on Intelligent Systems and Technology
related_work.relation: Most directly related work. Uses p-values + BH-Y correction for FDR control in PC. Our approach replaces p-values with e-values, eliminating the need for conservative BY correction and adding anytime validity.

related_work.title: False Discovery Rate Control with E-values
related_work.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society Series B
related_work.relation: Establishes the e-BH procedure we use for FDR control. We apply this framework to the new domain of causal graph discovery.

related_work.title: FDR Control for High-Dimensional Graphical Models via E-Values
related_work.relation: Applies e-values for FDR control in undirected Gaussian graphical models. We extend to directed causal graphs (DAGs/CPDAGs) with orientation rules.

related_work.title: Differentiable Constraint-Based Causal Discovery
related_work.authors: Jincheng Zhou, Mengbo Wang, Anqi He, Yumeng Zhou, Hessam Olya, Murat Kocaoglu, Bruno Ribeiro
related_work.relation: Also moves beyond binary CI decisions but via continuous relaxation and gradient optimization. Complementary approach; we maintain the combinatorial framework.

related_work.title: Causation, Prediction, and Search (2nd edition)
related_work.authors: Peter Spirtes, Clark Glymour, Richard Scheines
related_work.relation: Foundational reference for the PC and FCI algorithms that our method builds upon.

related_work.title: Hypothesis Testing with E-values
related_work.relation: Comprehensive treatment of e-value theory. Provides the statistical foundation for our CI test construction.

related_work.title: Empirically Calibrated Conditional Independence Tests
related_work.authors: Milleno Pan, Antoine de Mathelin, Wesley Tansey
related_work.relation: Addresses CI test calibration but not graph-level FDR control. Complementary to our approach.

related_work.title: Order-Independent Constraint-Based Causal Structure Learning
related_work.relation: PC-stable variant we use as the base algorithm for our E-PC implementation.

hypothesis: E-value-based conditional independence tests combined with the e-BH procedure will provide non-conservative graph-level FDR control in constraint-based causal discovery, recovering significantly more true edges than PC-p (which uses the conservative Benjamini-Yekutieli correction) at matched FDR levels, while maintaining valid FDR control under arbitrary dependence and supporting anytime stopping.
success_criteria: E-PC controls FDR within 10% of the nominal level across at least 80% of experimental configurations
E-PC recovers significantly more true edges than PC-p at matched FDR levels (p < 0.05 in paired comparison across datasets)
Anytime validity is verified: FDR control holds at every fold stopping point K' in {1, ..., K}
E-PC achieves competitive or superior SHD and F1 compared to standard PC at its best-tuned alpha
Results hold across both linear Gaussian and nonlinear data generating processes

computational_requirements.justification: Algorithm is combinatorial (not gradient-based), runs CI tests (correlation-based for linear, kernel-based for nonlinear) on moderate-sized datasets. Data splitting adds constant factor overhead. Largest experiments (p=200 nodes) may take ~1 hour each; parallelism across settings keeps total under 6 hours.
